text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
ON-TRIAL RESTAGING STUDY

CLINICAL INDICATION: 64-year-old female with Stage IIIB EGFR L858R-mutant lung adenocarcinoma (left upper lobe primary, T3 N3 M0 at diagnosis). Prior treatment history includes definitive concurrent chemoradiation (carboplatin/paclitaxel with 60 Gy IMRT), osimertinib consolidation/maintenance (discontinued at disease progression at approximately 14 months), two brain metastases treated with stereotactic radiosurgery, second-line carboplatin/pemetrexed x6 cycles followed by pemetrexed maintenance. Currently enrolled on Phase I/II clinical trial of tepotinib (MET inhibitor, 500 mg daily) in combination with osimertinib (80 mg daily) for EGFR L858R/T790M-mutant NSCLC with acquired MET amplification. This study obtained approximately 8 weeks after trial drug initiation for first on-trial restaging. Comparison: prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis obtained prior to trial enrollment (at completion of pemetrexed maintenance bridging).

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis performed with intravenous contrast (120 mL Omnipaque 350, power injected). Chest acquired in the portal venous phase; abdomen and pelvis acquired in the hepatic venous/equilibrium phase. Images reconstructed at 1.25 mm slice thickness in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes. Lung windows, mediastinal windows, and bone windows reviewed.

---

FINDINGS:

THORAX:

Lungs and Airways: The residual left upper lobe opacity/mass — representing the previously treated primary site — has undergone meaningful interval decrease in size. It now appears as an irregularly marginated soft tissue opacity measuring approximately 1.2 x 0.9 cm in axial dimensions (previously 1.8 x 1.4 cm on the pre-trial scan). The lesion retains a somewhat spiculated margin, partially surrounded by and blending into the adjacent post-radiation fibrotic scarring in the left upper lobe. There is persistent radiation-induced parenchymal fibrosis in the surrounding left upper lobe, manifesting as traction bronchiectasis, pleural tethering, and volume loss in the left upper lobe — findings that have been stable and are consistent with expected long-term sequelae of prior thoracic radiotherapy. The previously noted right lower lobe nodule is no longer identifiable on this study; it has either resolved completely or fallen below the threshold of confident measurement, which is consistent with response to therapy. No new pulmonary nodules. No endobronchial lesions. The central airways are patent. No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum and Hila: The right paratracheal (station 4R) lymph node cluster is now measuring approximately 0.7 cm in short axis, which appears slightly reduced or stable compared to prior. No pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy identified by size criteria. Post-treatment fibrotic/architectural distortion in the mediastinum and left hilum is unchanged from prior, consistent with prior radiation effect. Cardiac silhouette is within normal limits. No pericardial effusion. Thoracic aorta appears unremarkable. No superior vena cava compression.

Chest Wall and Pleura: No pleural thickening. No pleural nodularity. No rib erosion or cortical destruction. Visualized osseous structures of the thoracic cage show no suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions.

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is normal in size and contour with homogeneous parenchymal attenuation. No focal hepatic lesions identified. No biliary ductal dilation. Portal and hepatic veins patent.

Gallbladder and Biliary System: Gallbladder appears unremarkable without wall thickening or calculi. Common bile duct within normal limits.

Pancreas: The pancreas enhances homogeneously. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation. Peripancreatic fat is clean.

Spleen: Normal in size and attenuation. No focal splenic lesions.

Kidneys and Ureters: Both kidneys enhance symmetrically with normal corticomedullary differentiation. No hydronephrosis. No renal masses or calculi. No perinephric abnormality.

Adrenal Glands: The previously noted right adrenal nodule — measuring 0.7 cm at initial detection and 0.6 cm on the prior restaging study — is no longer identifiable on today's examination. The right adrenal gland appears normal in morphology without a discrete nodular focus. The left adrenal gland is unremarkable.

Gastrointestinal Tract: Stomach, small bowel, and visualized large bowel are unremarkable without wall thickening, obstruction, or mass. No free intra-abdominal air. No ascites.

Retroperitoneum and Lymph Nodes: No retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or iliac lymphadenopathy. No para-aortic nodal enlargement.

Abdominal Vasculature: Aorta normal in caliber without aneurysmal dilation. Celiac, SMA, and IMA appear patent.

PELVIS:

Pelvic Organs: Uterus is normal in size for patient age. No adnexal masses or free pelvic fluid. Bladder appears unremarkable.

Pelvic Lymph Nodes: No enlarged pelvic or inguinal lymph nodes.

OSSEOUS STRUCTURES: No suspicious lytic or blastic osseous lesions identified in the visualized spine, pelvis, or proximal long bones. Mild degenerative changes at the lumbar facet joints and intervertebral discs, age-appropriate. No cortical destruction.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. LEFT UPPER LOBE PRIMARY SITE: Meaningful interval decrease in the residual soft tissue opacity at the primary tumor site, now measuring approximately 1.2 x 0.9 cm compared to 1.8 x 1.4 cm on the pre-trial scan. The lesion has approximately one-third smaller in its cross-sectional dimensions and continues to be set within stable post-radiation fibrotic parenchyma. Findings are consistent with a substantive partial response to the current clinical trial regimen of tepotinib plus osimertinib.

2. RIGHT LOWER LOBE NODULE: Complete resolution of the previously documented right lower lobe nodule (0.9 cm at time of progression, 0.5 cm on the post-chemotherapy scan). No longer identifiable on today's study.

3. RIGHT ADRENAL NODULE: Previously documented right adrenal nodule is no longer identifiable. The adrenal gland appears normal. Complete resolution consistent with response to therapy.

4. NO NEW SITES OF DISEASE: No new pulmonary nodules, mediastinal adenopathy, hepatic lesions, or osseous lesions identified. No new abdominal or pelvic metastatic foci.

5. STABLE POST-TREATMENT CHANGES: Persistent left upper lobe radiation fibrosis and mediastinal architectural distortion are stable and expected given history of prior thoracic IMRT.

6. MEDIASTINAL LYMPH NODES: Right paratracheal nodal cluster appears stable to minimally decreased; no enlarging nodal disease.

Overall, the imaging findings reflect a meaningful and encouraging response to the combination tepotinib/osimertinib trial regimen, with shrinkage of the primary site lesion, resolution of the right lower lobe pulmonary nodule, and resolution of the right adrenal nodule. No evidence of new or progressive systemic disease on this examination.
